data.title: SA Asks: Which chip stocks are most attractive right now?
data.description:
Which semiconductor stocks are most attractive right now?

Seeking Alpha analysts Brett Ashcroft Green and KM Capital gave us their picks.

Brett Ashcroft Green: Chip stocks remain undervalued as a group, with iShares Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SOXX) and VanEck Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SMH) both in bear market territory. 

I, personally, like SOXX (NASDAQ:SOXX) as it has less concentration at the top than SMH (NASDAQ:SMH). As for individual names, I’m sticking with the two that have the largest moats and expected forward growth rates, Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and ASML (NASDAQ:ASML). 

Equipment names as a whole look like the best overall play in chips right now, with Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) and KLA (NASDAQ:KLAC) also looking like good values, despite their lower growth rates compared to ASML (NASDAQ:ASML). Their machines all deal with different stages of the semiconductor production process, and all will get business in the U.S. semi fab build outs that are in the pipeline.

KM Capital: While I recently upgraded my rating for AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) due to the extremely attractive valuation, I still think that over the long-haul they will struggle to keep up with Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) pace of innovation. 

Nvidia (NVDA) remains my top chip stock pick due to its unparalleled pricing power backed by aggressive innovation, which ensures best-in-class profitability and FCF generating capacity. With much higher scale and profitability compared to AMD (NASDAQ:AMD), Nvidia (NVDA) also has many more resources to invest in growth. In addition, Nvidia (NVDA) is not just a chip company but represents a diversified AI ecosystem and boasts a circa 90% GPU market share. With all these unrivaled positives being mentioned, I think that NVDA (NVDA) is an absolute steal at this share price given the FY2028 estimated forward P/E ratio of below 20.  

I'd also pick Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) because Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) does not directly compete with NVDA (NVDA) but is also a vital wide-moat player providing a rich variety of semiconductor products for AI data centers beyond GPUs or CPUs.

  • Top Semiconductor Stocks

data.title: Alamo Group outlines growth prospects with improved margins and strong industrial equipment performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Alamo Group Inc (ALG) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jeffrey Leonard highlighted the strong performance of the Industrial Equipment Division, which achieved 12.5% organic growth in net sales compared to Q1 2024, driven by robust demand for vacuum trucks, excavators, and snow removal equipment. The division's operating income reached $31.2 million, or 13.7% of net sales, reflecting a 120 basis point improvement in margin.
  • The Vegetation Management Division faced a 26.8% drop in net sales year-over-year but showed sequential improvement in profitability due to cost reduction measures implemented in 2024. Bookings for the division increased by nearly 18% compared to Q1 2024, and order cancellations were at their lowest since the start of 2024.
  • CFO Agnes Kamps reported Q1 2025 revenue of $391 million and a gross profit margin of 26.3%, a 10 basis point increase year-over-year. Operating income improved by 40 basis points to 11.4% of net sales. Net income was $31.8 million, or $2.64 per diluted share.
  • CFO Kamps noted significant debt reduction achievements, with total debt at $216.8 million and net debt at $16.5 million, a 91.7% improvement year-over-year. Operating cash flow for the quarter was $14.2 million.
  • CEO Leonard emphasized the company's acquisition strategy, describing the M&A market as the "most active" in recent years, with several promising opportunities being pursued.

Outlook

  • CEO Leonard expressed optimism for sustained growth in the Industrial Equipment Division, supported by a strong backlog of $513 million, up 6.6% sequentially.
  • The Vegetation Management Division is expected to continue its recovery, with margin improvements driven by efficiency measures and increasing orders.
  • Management remains focused on long-term growth and operational efficiency, expecting further sequential improvements in sales and margins across both divisions through the remainder of 2025.
  • The company reiterated its commitment to shareholder returns, with a quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share approved by the board.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 revenue totaled $391 million, down 8% year-over-year but reflecting a modest sequential improvement from Q4 2024.
  • Gross margin improved slightly to 26.3%, while SG&A expenses were reduced by 10% year-over-year due to efficiency initiatives.
  • Operating income increased by 40 basis points to 11.4% of net sales. Net income was $31.8 million, or $2.64 per diluted share, compared to $32.1 million or $2.67 per diluted share in Q1 2024.
  • The Vegetation Management Division reported a 27% decline in net sales year-over-year but achieved significant sequential improvements in operating margin and EBITDA. The Industrial Equipment Division achieved record net sales of $227.1 million and operating income of $31.2 million.

Q&A

  • Christopher Moore, CJS Securities, asked about tariff impacts and production adjustments. CEO Leonard explained that reciprocal tariffs pose a minor risk but are being mitigated by shifting production between facilities in the U.S. and Canada.
  • Linda Umwali, D.A. Davidson, inquired about operating margin improvements. CFO Kamps attributed the gains to cost reduction actions and facility consolidations, which are expected to provide further benefits.
  • Questions about M&A priorities and potential share repurchases were addressed by CEO Leonard, who emphasized the company's focus on acquisitions, describing the pipeline as "very active."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on tariff impacts, vegetation management recovery, and M&A opportunities. Their tone was generally neutral to slightly positive.
  • Management demonstrated confidence during the prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic progress and operational improvements. CEO Leonard's responses during the Q&A were detailed and reassured analysts about the company's resilience and growth potential.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts reflected increased optimism, particularly regarding the Vegetation Management Division's recovery and the Industrial Equipment Division's sustained strength.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue and gross margin improved sequentially, while operating income rose by 29% compared to Q4 2024.
  • The Vegetation Management Division showed sequential recovery in bookings and operating margin, supported by cost reductions and stabilization of market conditions.
  • The Industrial Equipment Division maintained strong performance with record net sales, driven by robust demand across product lines.
  • Management's tone was more confident compared to the previous earnings call, reflecting progress in cost reduction initiatives and a positive outlook for the remainder of 2025.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff impacts remain a concern, particularly reciprocal tariffs that could increase material costs.
  • Vegetation Management Division still faces market headwinds, including cautious dealer restocking and uncertainty in global trade.
  • Analysts raised questions about potential demand impacts from inflation and geopolitical uncertainties.

Final Takeaway

Alamo Group's Q1 2025 earnings call highlighted the company's focus on operational efficiency and strategic growth. The Industrial Equipment Division delivered strong results with record net sales and margin improvement, while the Vegetation Management Division showed signs of recovery supported by cost reduction measures. Management's confidence in the company's position and growth prospects was reinforced by its active M&A strategy and commitment to shareholder returns.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: First Phosphate announces $1M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• First Phosphate (OTCPK:FRSPF) announced a non-brokered private placement for gross proceeds of a minimum of $1M.
  • The proposed offering will include flow-through shares and hard dollar units, both priced at $0.35 per share.
  • Each hard dollar unit includes one common share and half a warrant, with full warrants exercisable at $0.50 until December 31, 2025, subject to early expiry.
  • The gross proceeds from the flow-through offering will be used to incur "Canadian exploration expenses" that are "flow-through mining expenditures" related to FRSPF's projects in Québec.
  • The net proceeds received from the hard dollar unit offering will be used for exploration and development activities, working capital and for general corporate purposes.
  • The proposed offering is expected to close, in one or more tranches, on or before May 27, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: U.S. stocks finish slightly lower ahead of possible weekend China trade deal
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages ended the week slightly lower Friday, losing gains for the week amid renewed optimism about upcoming trade deals and economic partnerships with other countries.

Both the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finished -0.1%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was unchanged, and the blue-chip Dow Jones (DJI) was last -0.3%.

Week-to-date, the S&P was -0.5%, the Dow was -0.2%, and the Nasdaq was -0.3%.

Over to the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 1 basis point to 4.39%, and the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was also up 1 basis point to 3.89%.

Markets did not advance on Friday, despite closing higher on Thursday, after President Donald Trump announced the trade agreement with the U.K. 

“The market this week has spoken clearly – it is, in our view, ready to rip,” Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.

“The most minor of news on the trade policy front (the agreement-to-agree with a country with which the US has a trade surplus) sent prices mooning before a brief respite. We believe the lows for the year are in and that new all-time highs are in the market’s sights. Bitcoin leads the way,” King added.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and other U.S. officials are expected to meet with high-level Chinese officials in Switzerland to start talks on trade. President Donald Trump suggested that reducing the current 145% tariff to 80% "seems right."

"Many Trade Deals in the hopper, all good (GREAT!) ones!" he posted on Truth Social, Friday morning.

Furthermore, among Friday's top gainers were Insulet (PODD), 20.8%, and Microchip Technology (MCHP), 11.6%; while among the top decliners were Akamai Technologies (AKAM), -10.8%, and Expedia (EXPE), -7.3%.

data.title: United States Antimony projects $40M-$50M revenue for 2025, plans Alaska antimony shipments by Q3
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: United States Antimony Corporation (UAMY) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Gary C. Evans highlighted significant year-over-year growth, with a 128% increase in revenues to $7 million and a 302% rise in gross profit, resulting in net income exceeding $0.5 million. He pointed out that Q1 2025 revenues were nearly 50% of the total reported for fiscal 2024.
  • CFO Rick Isaak detailed that the antimony business contributed $5.9 million in sales, a 140% increase due to strong demand and higher sales prices, though logistical delays impacted Q1 volumes. Zeolite sales also rose by $500,000, driven by increased demand and improved operations. Gross margin improved to 34%, up from 19% in the prior year.
  • Joe Bardswich, EVP and Chief Mining Engineer, discussed expanded exploration efforts in Alaska and Canada, with plans to begin trenching and stockpiling antimony ore by late summer. He emphasized the strategic advantage of transitioning from international to domestic antimony sourcing.
  • Jonathan Miller, VP of Investor Relations, announced enhancements in market visibility and shareholder engagement, including a tightened revenue guidance range of $40 million to $50 million for 2025.

Outlook

  • CEO Evans revised 2025 revenue guidance to $40 million to $50 million, narrowing the previous range. He confirmed plans to initiate antimony shipments from Alaska to Thompson Falls by late Q3, targeting September.
  • Expansion at the Thompson Falls smelter remains on schedule for completion by year-end, promising a sixfold increase in capacity. Evans indicated this would significantly enhance operational throughput and margin performance.

Financial Results

  • The company reported $7 million in revenue for Q1 2025, supported by strong contributions from the antimony and zeolite businesses. Antimony sales were $5.9 million, while zeolite sales contributed $1.1 million.
  • Gross profit increased to $2.4 million, with a margin improvement to 34%. This growth was attributed to higher sales prices and cost efficiencies, although higher antimony ore costs partially offset gains.
  • Operating income reached $358,000, a turnaround from a $470,000 loss in the previous year’s Q1. However, $750,000 in costs related to new management hires and $315,000 in project costs were noted as significant expenditures.

Q&A

  • Analyst: Your plans for the Thompson Falls expansion seem ambitious. How are you ensuring completion by year-end without disrupting current operations? Gary C. Evans: "We are confident the project will finish on time, leveraging a trusted contractor with extensive Montana experience. The expansion is designed as an add-on and will not impact existing operations."
  • Analyst: Can you clarify the timeline for Alaska ore shipments? Evans: "We’re targeting late summer, potentially as early as late July or August, depending on permitting and logistics."
  • Analyst: Will winter weather impact trucking operations in Alaska? Evans: "We’re stockpiling ore in accessible locations to ensure year-round trucking, even during harsh winter months."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed cautious optimism, focusing on operational execution and the Alaska project timeline. Questions centered on logistics, expansion, and market positioning.
  • Management demonstrated confidence, particularly in prepared remarks, using phrases such as "we are confident" and "we are working feverishly to accomplish our goals." However, during Q&A, the tone shifted to a more defensive posture when addressing logistical and permitting challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was more assertive about operational improvements and future prospects, while analysts maintained a similar level of skepticism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased significantly from Q4 2024, reflecting strong demand in both antimony and zeolite businesses. Gross margin also showed a notable improvement.
  • Management’s tone shifted to a more confident outlook, emphasizing progress in domestic sourcing and smelter expansion.
  • Analysts continued to focus on logistical challenges and the feasibility of achieving ambitious timelines, mirroring concerns from the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Analysts raised concerns about logistical issues related to Alaska ore shipments and winter trucking challenges.
  • Management acknowledged cost pressures from new initiatives and higher ore prices but emphasized cost control and operational efficiencies as mitigation strategies.
  • Delays in permitting or construction for the Thompson Falls smelter could impact planned capacity increases.

Final Takeaway

United States Antimony Corporation reported significant growth in Q1 2025, driven by strong performance in the antimony and zeolite segments. Management outlined ambitious plans for domestic antimony sourcing, with Alaska shipments expected by late Q3 2025. The company also revised its full-year revenue guidance to $40 million to $50 million, reflecting confidence in ongoing initiatives. Analysts remain cautious, focusing on execution risks and logistical challenges, but management’s proactive strategies aim to address these concerns while positioning the company for further growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Omega Healthcare Investors snaps six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Omega Healthcare Investors (NYSE:OHI) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses, as the stock closed 0.84% higher at $35.85 on Friday.

The company lost over 7.8% in the preceding six sessions.

OHI is down nearly 4% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.31% lower on Thursday at $35.55.

The stock has lost more than 5% so far this year, compared to nearly 4% decline in the broader S&P 500 Index.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, OHI has a Buy rating with a score of 4.43 out of 5. The company received A- in the prospect of valuation and profitability, while it got C in the growth factor.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and rated the stock as a Buy.

“Despite recent challenges in the nursing home sector, Omega's diversified portfolio and strategic acquisitions position it for future growth,” argued seeking Alpha analyst Investor Overview, adding that, “the company's AFFO is growing, and management has revised profit forecasts upwards, indicating a healthier financial outlook and potential for future dividend growth.”

Turning to the Wall Street community, Seven analysts gave OHI a Buy and above, and 10 analysts gave a Hold rating. No analyst gave Sell or Strong Sell rating.
